proposed_base_material_variables.configurational_entropy: The calculated theoretical mixing entropy of the alloy, typically expressed as a multiple of the gas constant (R), used to thermodynamically classify and evaluate high-entropy and medium-entropy alloys.
proposed_base_material_variables.pitting_resistance_equivalent_number: A theoretical index value (PRE or PREN) calculated from the base material's chemical composition (e.g., Cr, Mo, W, and N content) used to predict and rank relative resistance to localized pitting corrosion.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.sample_geometry_and_dimensions: The specific physical shape, cut dimensions, and orientation of the conditioned metal specimen prepared for electrochemical testing, which dictates the bulk size prior to defining the exposed active area.

proposed_experiment_variables.electrolyte_ph: The measured initial and/or final pH of the testing solution, representing the hydrogen ion concentration which dictates the thermodynamic stability of passive oxide films, localized repassivation kinetics, and specific active dissolution species.
proposed_experiment_variables.repassivation_potential: The protection potential (Erep) identified during cyclic potentiodynamic polarization at which the hysteresis loop closes and active localized pitting repassivates and arrests.
proposed_experiment_variables.corrosion_potential: The free or open-circuit resting potential (Ecorr or Eoc) of the alloy in the electrolyte measured at equilibrium prior to applying an external polarization sweep.
proposed_experiment_variables.counter_electrode: The specific material and structural form of the auxiliary electrode (e.g., platinum sheet, graphite rod) used in the multi-electrode electrochemical cell to complete the electrical circuit during testing.
